perturbation.original_text: If the security requirements for accessing, handling, and storing VA data and systems are specified in the contract, the contractor will comply with the contractual security requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: If the security requirements for accessing, handling, and storing VA data and systems are specified in the contract, the contractor may, at its discretion, comply with the contractual security requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing 'will comply' to 'may, at its discretion, comply.' This creates uncertainty about the enforcement of security requirements within the same section. It contradicts the overall intent of the contract, which mandates compliance with security protocols to protect sensitive VA information. This in-text contradiction in Section 3(a) undermines the obligatory nature of security compliance. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding how and whether security requirements should be enforced, undermining the purpose of the agreement. This also contradicts section 3(j) by creating ambigious discretion
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor, its employees, and its subcontractors and their employees will cooperate with VA and any law enforcement authority response for the investigation and prosecution of any possible criminal law violation(s) associated with any unauthorized access, disclosure, disposition, or destruction of VA property. The contractor will also cooperate with VA in any civil litigation to recover VA property, to obtain monetary or other compensation for a third party, or to obtain injunctive relief against any third party who accessed, modified, disclosed, or destroyed VA sensitive data and computer systems except as authorized under the contract or this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'will comply' with 'may, at its discretion, comply.' This introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates compliance with security requirements. The changed text makes compliance optional. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which requires the contractor to cooperate with VA and law enforcement in cases of unauthorized access or disclosure. Making security compliance discretionary directly undermines the enforceability of the cooperation clause, as the contractor might argue they are not obligated to follow security protocols in the first place. This aligns with the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction by providing conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The contractor will take reasonable steps to ensure that it will allow only those contractor and subcontractor employees who need to see the VA materials in order to perform the contract to do so.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor will take reasonable steps to ensure that it will allow contractor and subcontractor employees to see the VA materials in order to perform the contract.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the word 'only those' from Section 3(d) introduces an in-text contradiction with the preceding sentence. It changes the meaning from restricting access to VA materials to only necessary personnel to potentially allowing broader access, thereby contradicting the purpose of limiting access to sensitive information. This change introduces an in-text contradiction within the section concerning the scope of access to VA information and compromises the security of sensitive data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor, the contractor's employees, and any subcontractor and subcontractor's employees will access the VA information, software, applications, computer systems, and hardware which VA provides, or provides access to, only to the extent necessary, and only for the purpose of performing the contract. The contractor will take reasonable steps to ensure that it will allow contractor and subcontractor employees to see the VA materials in order to perform the contract. However, this agreement also applies to any other VA systems or data to which the contractor may have access to or be disclosed to the contractor.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the contractor will allow 'only those' contractor and subcontractor employees who need to see the VA materials to do so. The changed text removes 'only those', meaning that the contractor will allow contractor and subcontractor employees to see the VA materials, without specifying any restrictions. The contradicted text states that access is 'only to the extent necessary, and only for the purpose of performing the contract'. The changed text now contradicts the contradicted text, because it could allow for broader access than 'only to the extent necessary'.

perturbation.original_text: The contractor and its employees shall not make any copies of any VA information, including software or applications that are not copyrighted, except as necessary to restore VA computer systems or data storage devices to an operating state.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor and its employees shall make copies of any VA information, including software or applications that are not copyrighted.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'except as necessary to restore VA computer systems or data storage devices to an operating state' from Section 3(f), this perturbation creates an in-text contradiction. Now, the clause mandates the making of copies of VA information without any limitations, directly contradicting the implied restriction on copying earlier in the contract. This contradiction introduces ambiguity in the obligations of the contractor regarding VA property and is in-text contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor and its employees shall make copies of any VA information, including software or applications that are not copyrighted. Any copies made by the contractor or subcontractor shall be identified as VA property and handled as sensitive information under this non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricted the making of copies of VA information to only when necessary to restore VA computer systems or data. The changed text removes this restriction and mandates the making of copies of VA information without any limitations. This directly contradicts the original implied restriction, creating an inconsistency and contradiction within the contract, where the new obligation directly conflicts with the previously understood limitations. The contradiction is strong because the new obligation completely removes the prior restriction.

perturbation.original_text: The contractor may not use or disclose it except as the contractor is permitted to use or disclose VA sensitive information under the contract and this nondisclosure agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor may use or disclose it as the contractor sees fit.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 3(g) by removing the limiting clause 'except as the contractor is permitted to use or disclose VA sensitive information under the contract and this nondisclosure agreement' and replacing it with 'as the contractor sees fit' creates a direct conflict with the confidentiality provisions outlined in the contract. It suggests the contractor has unlimited discretion in using or disclosing VA information, contradicting the core intent of the NDA. This in-text contradiction introduces significant ambiguity and undermines the purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any information that the contractor and its employees learns about VA data and VA computer systems shall not be recorded except to the extent necessary to perform the contract, and such information, whether recorded or not, shall be handled as sensitive information under this agreement. The contractor may use or disclose it as the contractor sees fit.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the contractor's use or disclosure of sensitive information to only what was permitted under the contract and nondisclosure agreement. The changed text, "The contractor may use or disclose it as the contractor sees fit," removes this limitation, granting the contractor unfettered discretion. The contradicted text, found in the same location, is now in direct conflict with the changed text. The changed text implies the contractor has unlimited use and disclosure rights, while the contradicted text initially stated that information 'shall not be recorded except to the extent necessary to perform the contract, and such information, whether recorded or not, shall be handled as sensitive information under this agreement.' Both clauses now occupy the same space and are in direct opposition. This change creates a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because there are conflicting obligations within the same location, creating uncertainty about the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Prior to any disclosure pursuant to a court order, the contractor shall promptly notify VA of the court order and provide VA with a copy by fax or e-mail, whichever is faster, and notify by telephone the VA individual designated in advance to receive such notices.
perturbation.changed_text: The contractor is not required to notify VA of a court order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the phrase of 'Prior to any disclosure pursuant to a court order, the contractor shall promptly notify VA of the court order and provide VA with a copy by fax or e-mail, whichever is faster, and notify by telephone the VA individual designated in advance to receive such notices.' to 'The contractor is not required to notify VA of a court order' creates an in-text contradiction within the contract. This change now states that no notification is needed, directly contradicting the original intent of requiring prompt notification before disclosing information under a court order. It causes confusion regarding the contractor's obligations and conflicts with legal and compliance expectations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractor may disclose VA sensitive information to persons other than in the performance of this contract as authorized by the contract and this agreement in only two situations: (1) pursuant to an order of a court of competent jurisdiction; or (2) with VA's prior written authorization. The contractor is not required to notify VA of a court order. Prior to any disclosure pursuant to a court order, the contractor shall promptly notify VA of the court order and provide VA with a copy by fax or e-mail, whichever is faster, and notify by telephone the VA individual designated in advance to receive such notices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, 'The contractor is not required to notify VA of a court order,' directly contradicts the original text's requirement for prompt notification before disclosure under a court order. The contradicted text includes both the changed text and the original text, which highlights this in-text contradiction. Since the changed text removes the obligation to notify VA, it creates a clear conflict within the document, leading to uncertainty about the contractor's obligations and conflicting with legal and compliance expectations, as stated in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor will immediately deliver all VA records, data, copies of VA records and data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor, in its possession or the possession of any subcontractors, to the VA official designated in the contract or pursuant to this agreement. The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor may retain copies of VA records, data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 3(k) to state that the contractor 'may retain copies of VA records, data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor' directly contradicts the original clause which states 'The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.' This alteration creates significant ambiguity about the handling of VA information post-contract and weakens security protocols. This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding whether or not records can be retained, directly violating the intent of the NDA and also creates an in-text contradiction due to the first portion of the same sentence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon completion or termination of the contract for any reason, the contractor may retain copies of VA records, data, software and equipment, and information about VA data and systems recorded or documented by the contractor, in its possession or the possession of any subcontractors, to the VA official designated in the contract or pursuant to this agreement. The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the contractor 'may retain copies of VA records, data, software and equipment...', while the contradicted text, located in the same section, states 'The contractor will not retain any copies of any of these documents.' This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding whether the contractor is allowed to retain copies, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition because different sections (even within the same sentence now due to the change) provide conflicting obligations.
